title: Washington and Pharma Are Helping China Win
summary: Former Democratic Party Senator from Michigan, Debbie Stabenow, warns that the PRC is running its familiar economic aggression playbook to dominate pharmaceutical development as it did in other sectors. She urges the FDA to stop accepting uninspected Chinese trial data and Congress to add branded pharmaceuticals to investment restrictions before America's access to medicines becomes Beijing's next chokepoint.

title: Those Anti-Indian Posts and the “Great Replacement” Theory
summary: The Dean of RSIS in Singapore writes that a campaign of PRC-linked online posts claiming Indians were "overrunning" Chinese-majority Singapore exemplify how the Great Replacement conspiracy theory, which has fueled extremism in other parts of the world, is being adapted to local contexts. He observes that Singapore must defend its multicultural social cohesion against such hostile information campaigns through law,...

title: China Is Abusing AI: Chatbots are deftly disseminating Beijing’s talking points.
summary: While attention focuses on which country will dominate AI development, the Chinese Communist Party is already exploiting AI tools to run covert propaganda campaigns, sharpen domestic censorship, and skew chatbot outputs toward the Party's narratives, turning a technology once hoped to democratize information into an instrument of authoritarian influence.

title: Russia approved secret China military training at top level, sources say
summary: According to European officials and documents seen by Reuters, the PRC conducted covert training of Russian forces, which was personally approved by Russia's defense minister. It involved at least four Russian and Chinese generals and covered nuclear, biological, and chemical weapons. This signals that strategically significant military cooperation between Beijing and Moscow is accelerating despite denials by...

title: The EU should ban all trade with Israeli settlements
summary: It is high time the EU follow its own trade policy and ban trade of all goods and services from Israel’s illegal settlements in the West Bank

title: Former Senator Jeff Flake remembers his colleague and friend Lindsay Graham
summary: Former Republican Senator Jeff Flake served in congress alongside Lindsay Graham from 2013-2019. Despite moments of tension and disagreement, he says he and Sen. Graham were "friends to the end."

title: How longtime GOP Senator Lindsey Graham went from critic to ally of President Trump
summary: Longtime Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham has died at the age of 71. The foreign policy hawk and former critic-turned-ally of President Trump was running for re-election in South Carolina.

title: Politics chat: Lindsey Graham dies at 71, Congress back in session, U.S. strikes Iran
summary: South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham's sudden death at 71 has shocked Washington. Once a fierce critic of then-candidate Donald Trump, he became one of the president's most consistent defenders.

title: Campaign text messages could soon get more effective — and annoying
summary: Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages, making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.

title: An artist brought 'I.C.E. pops' to a Texas campus. The show was shut down in days
summary: The Trump administration's executive orders have meant that administrators are questioning what art can — and can't — be seen on campus.

title: McConnell says a fall led to his hospitalization, breaking weeks of silence about health condition
summary: McConnell, 84, explained the long silence about his condition by saying that "folks of my generation often hesitate to share the vulnerability that comes with growing older."

title: From staunch critic to fierce ally: Graham's long, strange and consequential friendship with Trump
summary: After the Capitol riot on Jan. 6, 2021, Sen. Lindsey Graham said he had finally had enough of the man who was championed by the mob that stormed the pillar of American democracy: President Donald Trump.

title: Lindsey Graham, longtime Republican senator, dies at 71 after 'brief and sudden illness'
summary: The statement his office posted on social media did not provide any additional details about the circumstances surrounding the Saturday night death of the South Carolina Republican, a former Air Force lawyer who served in Congress for three decades.

title: After Weeks of Silence, McConnell Says He Is Recovering From a Fall
summary: The Republican senator denied that he had suffered a heart attack and said he had left the hospital and moved to a physical rehabilitation center. He did not give a timetable for returning to Capitol Hill.

title: With Lindsey Graham Gone, Ukraine Loses an Ally in Trump’s Ear

summary: Senator Lindsey Graham died on Saturday night, a day after returning from Ukraine and four months before he was to face re-election.

summary: This report provides a strategic framework for assessing the conditional value of artificial intelligence partnerships across different technological futures and presents options for transatlantic cooperation that advance U.S. strategic interests.

title: Title IX’s Failed Experiment: Why Accommodating Sex Differences Beats Engineered Parity
summary: Introduction In 2024, a Tunisian man defeated a Chinese woman for the Olympic women’s boxing gold medal. The spectacle of a biological male pummeling a female athlete shocked many and led to renewed calls to keep men out of women’s sports. title: Restoring Obscenity Regulation in an Internet Age
summary: Introduction Americans born after the mid-1990s have lived their entire lives in a world awash with hardcore pornography. title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus
summary: The authors identify the inventory of critical electrical equipment for artificial intelligence (AI) data center operations and the impact of supply chain vulnerabilities on the U.S. power grid’s ability to meet AI demand by 2030.

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.
